_comment: Guardian Discord Bot egg — compatible with both Pterodactyl and Pelican (same egg schema). Import via Admin -> Nests/Eggs -> Import Egg, or Import from URL.
name: Guardian Discord Bot
author: guardian@example.com
description: All-in-one Discord moderation, music, reaction-role, anti-bot captcha, backup/restore and welcome bot built on discord.js v14. Pulls from a Git repository, installs dependencies and runs automatically.
startup: export YT_DLP_PATH="$(pwd)/yt-dlp"; if [ -f "$YT_DLP_PATH" ]; then "$YT_DLP_PATH" -U || true; fi; if [ "${AUTO_UPDATE}" = "1" ] && [ -d .git ]; then git pull; fi; npm install --no-audit --no-fund && npm start

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Guardian Discord Bot installation script
# Installs build tooling, yt-dlp, clones the repository and installs dependencies.

apt-get update
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git make gcc g++ python3 curl ca-certificates

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

if [ -n "${GIT_REPO}" ]; then
    if [ -d .git ]; then
        echo "Existing repository found, pulling latest changes..."
        git pull
    else
        echo "Cloning ${GIT_REPO} (branch ${GIT_BRANCH})..."
        git clone --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_REPO}" .
    fi
else
    echo "No GIT_REPO set — skipping clone. Upload the bot files manually."
fi

echo "Downloading standalone yt-dlp (no Python required)..."
curl -fsSL https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/latest/download/yt-dlp_linux -o /mnt/server/yt-dlp
chmod +x /mnt/server/yt-dlp

if [ -f package.json ]; then
    echo "Installing npm dependencies..."
    export YOUTUBE_DL_SKIP_DOWNLOAD=true
    npm install --no-audit --no-fund
fi

mkdir -p /mnt/server/data
echo "Guardian installation complete."

variables.name: Discord Bot Token
variables.description: Your Discord bot token from https://discord.com/developers/applications (Bot -> Token). Keep this secret.

variables.name: Application (Client) ID
variables.description: Your application's Client ID (General Information -> Application ID). Used to register slash commands.

variables.name: YouTube API Key
variables.description: YouTube Data API v3 key for reliable music search. Optional but recommended.

variables.name: Spotify Client ID
variables.description: Spotify application Client ID (https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard) used to resolve Spotify links.

variables.name: Spotify Client Secret
variables.description: Spotify application Client Secret. Keep this secret.

variables.name: Database URL
variables.description: Database connection string. Default uses an embedded SQLite file. For PostgreSQL use: postgres://user:pass@host:5432/dbname

variables.name: Git Repository
variables.description: HTTPS URL of the Git repository to deploy from (used by the install script and auto-update).

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: Set to 1 to git pull the latest code on every start, or 0 to disable.

variables.name: Dashboard Enabled
variables.description: Enable the web configuration dashboard (true/false). When on, Guardian starts an HTTP server on the port below.

variables.name: Dashboard Port
variables.description: TCP port for the web dashboard. Assign a game server port in the panel and use its INTERNAL value here.

variables.name: Dashboard Public URL
variables.description: Public URL where the dashboard is reachable (used for OAuth redirect). Example: https://guardian.example.com  — must exactly match the redirect URI configured under OAuth2 in the Discord Developer Portal.

variables.name: Dashboard Session Secret
variables.description: Random long string used to sign session cookies. Generate one with: openssl rand -hex 48

variables.name: Discord Client Secret
variables.description: Discord application Client Secret (OAuth2 -> Client Secret). REQUIRED to enable the web dashboard login flow. Keep this secret.
